stories.title: New US Homeland Security chief faces criticism on both sides less than a month into tenure
stories.summary: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin is trying to bring his department out from under the shadow of his divisive predecessor Kristi Noem, but he is facing hits from both sides of the aisle as he does so. Less than a month into his tenure atop the Department of Homeland Security, the former Republican lawmaker from Oklahoma has attempted to stabilize it by getting rid of some of Noem’s management processes and reshaping the department’s organizational chart. In the past week, he  bro

stories.title: Spain pushes to end EU-Israel association agreement
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez on Sunday urged the EU to end its association agreement with Israel. In an escalation of his criticism against Israel, he said during a rally in Andalusia that “a government that violates international law or the principles of the EU cannot be its partner.” Spain will formally propose the termination of the agreement at a meeting of EU foreign ministers on Tuesday in Luxembourg. Sánchez has emerged as one of Israel’s most vocal critics in the EU. H

stories.title: Trump’s loud Orban support contrasts with decades of covert US electoral warfare
stories.summary: When Dwight Eisenhower wanted to ensure that Japanese voters stuck with U.S.-friendly prime minister Nobusuke Kishi in the 1958 elections over socialist and communist challengers, the clandestine machinery of the national-security state whirred into action. American spies recruited key officials within Japan ’ s governing Liberal Democratic Party to funnel inside information to the Central Intelligence Agency and arranged a secret meeting at a Tokyo hotel with a former prime minister to deliver

stories.title: Russia-aligned Rumen Radev set to win Bulgarian election
stories.summary: Rumen Radev, a Russia-aligned former president , looks on course for an emphatic win in Bulgaria’s election on Sunday, but will need to team up with other parties to form a governing majority. Radev’s newly created Progressive Bulgaria movement was on track to secure just under 40 percent of the vote, according to projections broadcast on television after the polls closed. The polling agency Market Links predicted his party would win 38.9 percent, while Alpha Research put him on 37.5 percent. Th

stories.title: Hungary’s Tisza party widens parliamentary majority as final votes are counted
stories.summary: Hungary’s Tisza party increased its parliamentary majority by three seats after the final votes were counted in last Sunday’s election. The latest results strengthen Hungarian Prime Minister-elect Péter Magyar’s mandate to dismantle changes made by outgoing leader Viktor Orbán during 16 years in power. The final tally gives Tisza 141 seats out of 199 in the Hungarian national assembly, up from 138 in previous projections. This result places the party well above the two-thirds s

stories.title: Orbán signals readiness to approve €90B EU loan to Ukraine once Druzhba oil is flowing
stories.summary: Hungary’s outgoing Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Sunday signaled readiness to lift his veto over a €90 billion EU loan to Ukraine before leaving office — if Kyiv restores the flow of oil to Hungary. In an unexpected development, Orbán said that he will approve the much-needed loan to Ukraine if Kyiv repairs Druzhba pipeline — which might happen as early as Monday. This could provide immediate relief for Ukraine’s war-battered economy as it seeks to defend itself from Russia’s full-sc

stories.title: Israel had a bad week in Europe. Does it herald a wider shift in EU relations?
stories.summary: With Orbán gone and Meloni distancing herself, EU sanctions on trade and settlers are looking more likely It was a bad week for Israel in Europe: the country lost its staunchest regional ally when Viktor Orbán was toppled from power in Hungary, and Italy suspended a key defence pact . The shifts are likely to pave the way for long-delayed sanctions against violent settlers in the occupied West Bank, and add to broader pressure for the EU to reconsider its relationship with Israel over its wars i

stories.title: Mass shooting rampage in Louisiana leaves eight children dead and others wounded
stories.summary: Suspect was fatally shot, according to Shreveport police, who say victims included children ranging from 18 months to 14 years Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox At least eight people are dead and others are wounded after a mass murder reported in the Louisiana city of Shreveport, according to local police . Police said that among the victims were children ranging in age from 18 months to 14 years. Two other women were reportedly shot in the head but su

stories.title: Labor accused of ignoring anti-racism plan as documents reveal repeated pleas to take action
stories.summary: The government has defended its response, citing the upcoming royal commission and work done by the Islamophobia and antisemitism special envoys Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Federal Labor has been accused of dragging its feet on a plan to combat systemic racism in Australia, nearly 18 months after it received recommendations from the human rights commission. New documents released to the Senate show no progress on the national strategy delivered to the government

stories.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
stories.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

stories.title: Australia’s coalmine emissions are increasing. Is this how a major policy to cut climate pollution is meant to work?
stories.summary: The Albanese government overhauled policy and promised significant pollution cuts – but carbon offsets are still being used as an excuse Sign up for climate and environment editor Adam Morton’s free Clear Air newsletter here Is this how a national scheme to cut climate pollution is supposed to work? Australian government data released this week shows emissions from Australian coalmines increased last financial year. About 80% of the coalmines pumped more into the atmosphere than their government

stories.title: Gyorsan túllépett a Kreml Orbánon
stories.summary: Hasznos volt Moszkvának az EU-val konfrontálódó Orbán, aki alárendelt pozíciót vett fel az oroszokkal szemben. Miután az új kormány sem akar minden gazdasági kapcsolatot elvágni, az orosz vezetés egy pillanat alatt átállt arra, hogy a Tisza-kabinet a pragmatikus kapcsolatokhoz alkalmas partner lesz. Magyar nyilatkozatait az ukrajnai háborúról pedig elengedték a fülük mellett.

stories.title: A sárvári körzetben induló Magyar Péter már egy éve feltűnt egy Lázárinfón, és ott volt a Békemeneten is
stories.summary: A független jelölt a helyiek szerint is elkötelezett fideszes, korábban több fideszes politikussal fotózkodott.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Magas szintű tárgyalásokat folytattunk az Európai Bizottság ide látogató vezetőivel
stories.summary: Az uniós forrás nem könyöradomány, hanem egy ellentételezés a magyarok által az Uniónak befizetett pénzekért és hazánk Európáért végzett munkájáért – írta a leendő miniszterelnök Facebookon.

stories.title: Balogh Levente reakciója arra, hogy Kocsis Máté árulónak nevezte: A Szentkirályi már akkor is létezett, amikor Orbán Viktor még azt sem tudta, hol van
stories.summary: A Szentkirályi Ásványvíz alapítója Kocsis Máté posztjára reagált, amiben őt is kritizálta. Azt mondta: szívesen elbeszélgetne erről Kocsis Mátéval a Telexen egy élő podcastben.

stories.title: 16 éve erre vártam, most mégis szorongok, mi a baj velem?
stories.summary: A választás óta milliók vannak sokkban, milliók ünnepelnek, és rengetegen vannak olyanok is, akik nem merik elhinni, le vannak fagyva, vagy épp végtelen fásultságot éreznek. Pedig épp olyasmi történt, amire nagyon régen vágytak. Pszichológusok magyarázzák el, miért lehet ez.

stories.title: Európa ezüstérmesei lettek a Pest megyeiek ingázásban
stories.summary: A pesti régióból ingázók aránya csaknem 7 százalékponttal nőtt 2015 és 2024 között. Az egész EU-ban csak egy régióban magasabb az ingázási arány a pesti 45 százaléknál.

stories.title: Az Europion felmérése szerint a magyarok több mint fele ért egyet azzal, hogy Magyar Péter a közjogi méltóságok lemondását követelje
stories.summary: A felmérésben arról is kérdezték a válaszadókat, mit gondolnak Magyar Péter közmédiás interjúiról.

stories.title: Jeszenszky Zsolt a vereség okairól: Orbán Indiában akaratán kívül kinyitott egy kaput a gonosz szellemek felé
stories.summary: De nem akármilyen kaput, mindjárt a szellemvilágét!

stories.title: Orbán szerint hétfőn újraindulhat a szállítás a Barátság kőolajvezetéken
stories.summary: Magyar Péter hétfői nemzetközi sajtótájékoztatóján Ukrajna 90 milliárdos hiteléről azt mondta, már decemberben döntés született erről és Magyarországra nem vonatkozik.

stories.title: A magyar Dávid küzdelme Googliáttal – egy építőmérnök harca a nyelvromboló androidos billentyűzet ellen
stories.summary: Rengetegen használják a Google saját billentyűzetét, pedig az mindent megtesz azért, hogy ne tudjunk magyarul helyesen írni. Egy magyar mérnök végül megelégelte ezt, és hetekig küzdött, hogy a techóriás lássa: több millió felhasználót kínoz, teljesen feleslegesen.

stories.title: Közel 92 milliárd forint érkezhet Magyarországra, Norvégia nyárig szeretne megállapodni az új kormánnyal
stories.summary: Magyarország a legutóbbi ciklusban egyetlen norvég koronát sem kapott a támogatásból, mivel a felek nem tudtak megegyezni abban, pontosan ki ossza szét és kapja meg a pénzt.

stories.title: Political Capital: Orbán a gyurcsányosodás útjára léphet
stories.summary: Szerintük az őszinte szembenézésnek eddig nem sok jele van a párt vezetésében, egyelőre Orbán Viktor pozíciójának mentése zajlik, de ez nem lesz elég.

stories.title: A Pesti Srácok főszerkesztője szerint azért bukott a Fidesz, mert nem változtatták meg 2024-ben a választási rendszert
stories.summary: Gajdics Ottó a Sajtóklubban arról beszélt, szerinte jóval korábban és határozottabban kellett volna fellépni, amikor „kiállították rólunk, hogy nem újságírók, hanem propagandisták vagyunk, akikre oda se kell figyelni”.

stories.title: Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations
stories.summary: Separately, Spirit Airlines reportedly seeks emergency US government funds as Iran war keeps aviation fuel costs high Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Air Canada has announced a temporary suspension of flights from Toronto and Montreal to New York ’s John F Kennedy airport, citing rising fuel prices. The move comes amid growing concerns that airlines worldwide may scale back services as aviation fuel costs climb in the wake of the US and Israel’s ongo

stories.title: Péter Magyar’s real coup was winning over loyal Orbán voters – not preaching to the converted
stories.summary: He is no progressive but unlike the old opposition, the Tisza leader listened to Orbán’s rural base The international audience observing the Hungarian election result is likely to settle on a view that feels familiar. That this election was about east v west or that it was a “youthquake”, a win secured by the unprecedented participation of young voters. These narratives have some truth to them, of course, but, especially for those interested in fighting back against regimes such as Viktor Orbán’

stories.title: Erdélyi és partiumi Tisza Szigetek képviselőit kérdeztük, mit gondolnak az RMDSZ korábbi politikájáról
stories.summary: A Tisza erdélyi szimpatizánsai szerint az RMDSZ legnagyobb hibája, hogy egyoldalúan a Fidesz mögé állt, háttérbe szorította a közösségen belüli más hangokat, és most ezért politikai felelősséget kellene vállalnia. Két erdélyi Tisza Sziget alapítóját kérdeztük a választás után: szerintük a kampány során történt visszaéléseket és a határon átnyúló pénzek körüli korrupciógyanút ki kell vizsgálni, mert ezekkel a reflexekkel nem lehet tovább működtetni a romániai magyar politikát.

stories.title: Orbán's era was over in a flash and Hungary's next PM is a man in a hurry
stories.summary: Péter Magyar and his Tisza party are wasting no time preparing for the transfer of power after their dramatic landslide victory.

stories.title: Hormuz standoff shows no signs of letting up for vital shipping to resume from Gulf
stories.summary: Maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz remained at minimal levels on Friday resulting in a giant parking lot, as the US-Iran standoff continues with the same cautious approach since Monday.

stories.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
stories.summary: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

stories.title: Battleground Republicans are starting to worry about the Senate
stories.summary: There’s a growing anxiety gnawing at battleground Republicans: Maybe their Senate majority isn’t as safe as they once thought. Democrats still face steep odds in their bid to flip the chamber, but interviews with nearly two dozen GOP operatives, party chairs and strategists across the country’s battlegrounds found a persistent concern that the longer the  Iran war drags on  and the economy sputters, the more it could complicate their path to keeping their majority in November. “Momentu

stories.title: Iran mocks EU calls to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The Iranian government ridiculed EU top diplomat Kaja Kallas’ calls to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and respect international law.   Naval traffic was at a standstill on Sunday after Iranian gunboats fired against vessels attempting to pass through the key waterway. Tehran backtracked from its earlier decision to open the Strait of Hormuz in response to a U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports, which threatens to stymie its oil exports. Western leaders have urged the Iranian re

stories.title: US-Iran talks to continue Monday in Pakistan, Trump says
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Sunday announced the U.S. will continue peace talks with Iranian representatives in Pakistan on Monday — even as he continued to threaten striking civilian infrastructure in the region. In  a post to social media , the president accused Iran of violating a ceasefire and striking multiple European ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Now, Trump said, “many” ships are headed to the U.S. “to load up.” “We’re offering a very fair and reasonable DEAL, and I hope they tak

stories.title: UAE official: More than 90% of Iranian targets were civilian infrastructure
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates’ minister of state said Sunday the country had been hit with over 2,800 missiles and drones in the first 40 days of the U.S. and Israel’s war with Iran, adding that more than 90% of the targets were civilian infrastructure. Reem Al Hashimy, the UAE’s minister of state for international cooperation, said during a Sunday morning appearance on ABC’s “This Week” that Iran was seeking to destroy the UAE’s “model of prosperity and tolerance. “We used our oil wealth to build an

stories.title: Several killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages – video report
stories.summary: Ukrainian investigators are examining whether a terrorist attack in Kyiv was directed by Moscow, after a man shot dead six people on Saturday before he was killed by police. The gunman, 58, was a Ukrainian citizen born in Moscow. He opened fire on passersby before barricading himself in a supermarket and taking hostages. Detectives sealed off the area in the Holosiivskyi district and tried to negotiate with him. He was killed after a 40-minute standoff Investigators examine whether Ukraine terro

stories.title: Rat poison found in baby food jar in Austria after product recall
stories.summary: Police say poison detected in jar of HiPP carrots and potatoes as maker says items may have been tampered with Rat poison has been found in a jar of HiPP baby food, police in Austria have said, after a recall of the product from more than 1,000 Spar supermarkets in the country over safety fears. Police in Burgenland said in a statement that a sample from one of the 190g (7oz) jars of carrots and potatoes baby food reported by a customer had tested positive for rat poison. Continue reading...

stories.title: Italian lawyers could win ‘wild west-style bounties’ if immigration clients go home
stories.summary: Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government to ask MPs to back controversial voluntary repatriation scheme Italian lawyers will be paid bonuses if they successfully convince their immigrant clients to return home under a government plan that has been compared to a “wild west-style bounty”. The incentive is in the latest security bill from Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government and goes to the lower house of parliament for final approval this week. It was passed by the upper house after fiery debate. Co

stories.title: Reform UK’s Richard Tice allegedly failed to pay £100,000 in corporation tax
stories.summary: Deputy leader ran shell companies that reportedly did not pay tax on profits from 2020 to 2022, during which time his firm donated £1.1m to party Richard Tice allegedly failed to pay almost £100,000 in corporation tax to the benefit of his investment company, which in turn made donations to Reform UK, it has been reported. In response to the report in the Sunday Times , the deputy leader of Reform UK posted a lengthy statement on X, in which he said: “A long career with multiple businesses is bo

stories.title: Trump energy secretary says gas prices might not drop back under $3 a gallon until 2027
stories.summary: Chris Wright says ‘I don’t know’ when asked about lower cost of gas as average price soars to $4 a gallon in US Chris Wright, the Trump administration ’s energy secretary, acknowledged Sunday that it might not be until 2027 before US gas prices come back under $3 a gallon. Asked by Jake Tapper, the CNN State of the Union host, when he thought “it’s realistic for Americans to expect the gas will go back to under $3 a gallon”, Wright replied: “I don’t know. That could happen later this year. That

stories.title: Ministers urged to share Mandelson vetting files with intelligence committee
stories.summary: Whitehall officials are weighing up ‘unprecedented’ disclosure of documents in security clearance scandal Starmer did not gamble with national security over Mandelson, says minister Ministers are under growing pressure to share the documents from Peter Mandelson’s vetting process with the parliamentary committee tasked with deciding if they should be made public. In February, MPs passed a binding parliamentary motion, known as a humble address, requiring the government to publish “all papers” re

stories.title: Trump sends delegation to Pakistan for possible new round of Iran war talks
stories.summary: Tehran reportedly ‘yet to decide’ if it will enter discussions as US president repeats threat to raze Iran’s infrastructure Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump’s representatives will return to Pakistan on Monday for another possible round of talks aimed at ending the US-Israeli war in Iran, after the US president repeated his threats to Iranian infrastructure unless Iran agrees to a deal. The return of a US delegation to Islamabad, led by vice-president JD Vance, along with Trump’s sp

stories.title: Party of Bulgaria’s pro-Russian former president ahead in exit polls
stories.summary: Rumen Radev’s centre-left Progressive Bulgaria group ran on anti-corruption platform in country’s eighth election in five years The party of Bulgaria’s pro-Russian former president Rumen Radev has come first in the country’s eighth parliamentary elections in five years, according to exit polls, but without securing a majority. Radev, who resigned as president in January, ran on a pledge to fight corruption after an anti-graft movement triggered a long political crisis. Continue reading...

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran reportedly rejects US peace talks as Trump threatens to ‘knock out’ all Iranian power plants
stories.summary: The US president said negotiators would head to Pakistan as Iran pledged to keep the strait of Hormuz closed until the US naval blockade is lifted Full report: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’ UN secretary-general António Guterres has strongly condemned the killing of a French peacekeeper and the wounding of three others in an attack in southern Lebanon , spokesman Stéphane Dujarric said in a statement to the Associated Press. The UN peacekeeping force came under atta

stories.title: Ben Roberts-Smith denies war crimes allegations – As it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Defence Minister Richard Marles says the success of Australia’s alliance with the United States should not be measured against a single president or federal government in Canberra, insisting the longstanding ties will endure. US President Donald Trump has repeatedly criticised Australia for not assisting with the war in Iran, while federal Labor, including Marles and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese , say the Whi

stories.title: German crime statistics: false blame on migrants?
stories.summary: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iranian bridges as talks set to continue
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said the US would destroy every bridge and power plant in Iran if Tehran does not accept a peace proposal. Negotiations are set to resume in Pakistan soon. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
stories.summary: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly. Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
stories.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why are gray whales dying in San Francisco's waters? US scientists search for clues
stories.summary: Climate change and shifting migration patterns are bringing rare research opportunities and new mysteries.

stories.title: Eight children killed in Louisiana shooting, police say
stories.summary: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana, which police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".

stories.title: More Britons opt to holiday in UK this summer amid uncertainty over flights
stories.summary: Holiday park firms say such bookings are on the rise because of impact of Iran war on aviation Holiday companies have predicted a surge in bookings for UK summer breaks after a jump in interest from Britons fearful of flight cancellations linked to the Iran war. Summer bookings are expected to rise in the coming weeks amid warnings of possible jet fuel shortages and resulting cancellations by airlines across Europe. Continue reading...

stories.title: Labour’s ‘crabwise’ approach to closer EU ties must address damage of Brexit | Heather Stewart
stories.summary: The damage to the economy dwarfs the upsides from the various non-EU trade deals the UK has struck since 2016 Rachel Reeves joined EU finance ministers for dinner in Washington last week, on the sidelines of the International Monetary Fund spring meetings – the first time a chancellor had done so since Brexit. It was the latest symbolic step in Labour’s marked shift towards prioritising closer EU relations. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian astronaut’s bon mots help heal wounds from French language row
stories.summary: Jeremy Hansen praised for speaking French in space after Air Canada chief’s linguistic snub exposed tensions and drew rebuke from PM Few people foresaw humanity’s quest for the moon as accurately as the 19th-century French author Jules Verne, whose two works –From the Earth to the Moon and Around the Moon – anticipated many of the features of modern lunar exploration. But Verne’s language had never been spoken in deep space until the Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen uttered four words during Nas

stories.title: Nathalie Baye, prolific star of French and Hollywood cinema, dies aged 77
stories.summary: Actor who worked with the great French auteurs in the 1970s and 80s and starred in Spielberg’s Catch Me if You Can died of Lewy body dementia, says family The French film star Nathalie Baye, who starred in a string of highly regarded French films as well as Steven Spielberg’s Catch Me If You Can, has died at the age of 77, her family said on Saturday. Baye, a stalwart of France’s domestic cinema, starred in about 80 films and took home the best actress César, France’s equivalent of the Oscars, f

stories.title: UK seeks EU deals on steel and EVs in push for closer economic ties
stories.summary: Agreements would aim to shield British industry from new steel tariffs and stricter rules on electric vehicles due in 2027 Downing Street hopes to secure deals on steel and electric cars with the EU as it seeks to upgrade the post-Brexit economic relationship. Amid economic uncertainty caused by the conflict in the Middle East and strains in relations with the US, Keir Starmer is seeking closer economic ties with the EU. Continue reading...

stories.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon
stories.summary: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world record More than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humans They can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons. In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tornadoes and heavy winds destroy homes and roads across US midwest
stories.summary: No deaths reported after latest round of severe weather in the region as officials brace residents for long recovery A trail of damaged homes and buildings dotted a wide swath of the US on Saturday after a burst of destructive winds and reported tornadoes tore off roofs, uprooted trees and rendered rural roads impassable with debris. No deaths were reported after Friday’s storms , which barreled through the upper midwest and delivered the latest round of severe weather to batter the region. Offi

stories.title: Obama and Mamdani read and sing with New York preschoolers in first meeting
stories.summary: Former US president and New York mayor read to a group of children and led a sing-along at a Bronx childcare center Barack Obama met with Zohran Mamdani for the first time on Saturday at a childcare center where the former Democratic US president and mayor of New York City read to preschoolers and led a sing-along. The meeting comes as Mamdani, a democratic socialist who marked his 100th day in office just over a week earlier, is also trying to build a working relationship with Donald Trump – Ob

stories.title: Intemperate Trump brings chaos and confusion to Iran talks
stories.summary: US president’s unreliable style sows diplomatic confusion but leaves Tehran clear on strategic value of strait of Hormuz Donald Trump’s decision to send US officials to Islamabad for further talks on Monday with Iran just 24 hours after Iran once again closed the strait of Hormuz will signal to Tehran that the strategic waterway remains a bargaining asset beyond parallel. It will also confirm in Iran’s eyes that the US president’s chaotic approach to diplomacy doubles the need for Tehran to act

stories.title: Civil resistance activists detained in Manchester over alleged plan to raid high-end stores
stories.summary: Take Back Power, which targets the super-rich, says seven members were arrested at a training session Seven people from an activist group calling for higher taxes on the super-rich have been arrested by police on suspicion of conspiracy to steal. Police confirmed that six women and one man were detained in Salford, Greater Manchester, on Sunday over what they said was a coordinated plan to steal from high-end stores. Continue reading...

stories.title: Starmer is facing his judgment day over Mandelson missteps
stories.summary: Ahead of a showdown with MPs, prime minister looks like a man who is not really in control in his own government Keir Starmer has spent much of the last 24 hours working on a plan for what senior government figures are already describing as his “judgment day”: his showdown with MPs on Monday over the latest Peter Mandelson revelations . That the prime minister was apparently not told of Mandelson’s vetting failure has provoked incredulity across Westminster and accusations he sacked a senior civ

stories.title: ‘Independent’ group Energy for Australians that ran anti-Labor ads received more than $1m from coal lobby
stories.summary: Coal Australia denies its donations to the ‘community-driven association’ amount to astroturfing, but critics accuse the group of misleading the public Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An “independent, community-driven association” that ran anti-Labor adverts during the last federal election was entirely funded by a coal industry lobby group, the Guardian can reveal. Energy for Australians accepted more than $1m f

stories.title: More than 3,400 Iranians killed in war - as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Our latest main story on the Middle East crisis is here. Separate to the Pakistani army chief’s trip to Iran (see post at 07:53 ), the Pakistani prime minister, Shehbaz Sharif, and foreign minister Ishaq Dar also concluded a trip to the Middle East after visiting Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkey for talks. “We have just concluded the last leg of our engagements following productive and fruitful visits … where we held meaningful bilateral discussions aimed at strengthening

stories.title: India protests Iran fire on two vessels in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: New Delhi summoned Iran's envoy after two India-flagged vessels were attacked while attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Attacks on US academics: A microcosm of a larger threat to democracy
stories.summary: In the new DW documentary "Democracy Under Attack: Can Dündar and Trump's America," the Turkish press freedom icon looks at the parallels between the erosion of democracy in the US and his home country.

stories.title: Robot runners beat humans in Beijing half-marathon
stories.summary: For the first time, humanoid robots have outclassed humans in a half-marathon held in Beijing. The winner beat the men's record for the distance set by Uganda’s Jacob Kiplimo in March.

stories.title: French billionaire defiant as authors quit top publisher
stories.summary: Vincent Bollore has vowed to find new authors for publisher Grasset after 170 left, alleging political interference. French President Macron has weighed in on the affair, which has shaken France's cultural scene.

stories.title: Malaysia: Fire in impoverished village destroys 1,000 homes
stories.summary: The fire ripped through makeshift homes in a "water village" in Malaysia's Sabah ‌state ⁠on ⁠Borneo island. The area is home to some of the country's poorest residents, including indigenous and stateless communities.

stories.title: India's harvest festivals under climate strain
stories.summary: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

stories.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
stories.summary: Iran says it has placed mines in the Straight of Hormuz to discourage independent traffic. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

stories.title: US mass shooting leaves 8 children dead in Louisiana
stories.summary: A "domestic disturbance" in the US state of Louisiana has claimed the lives of eight children, including a 1-year-old, police have said.

stories.title: Life in Donbas: 'If we give up, there will be nothing left'
stories.summary: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

stories.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russian Radev leading after parliamentary vote, exit polls show
stories.summary: Exit polls put Rumen Radev's new, left-leaning, pro-Russian party far ahead following Bulgaria's eighth election in five years. Radev, a former fighter pilot, stepped down as Bulgaria's president in January.

stories.title: Bayern Munich clinch 35th Bundesliga title
stories.summary: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

stories.title: Trump signs order to fast-track research on psychedelic drugs
stories.summary: Trump signed an executive order Saturday to ease research restrictions on psychedelic drugs like psilocybin and LSD, aiming to accelerate studies into their potential for treating mental health conditions.

stories.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.title: Bodies of 50 infants dumped at Trinidad graveyard
stories.summary: Police say a preliminary investigation shows it may be a case of an "unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses".

stories.title: China hosts humanoid robots in Beijing half marathon
stories.summary: China has tested humanoid robots in a real race setting in Beijing. The event highlights advances in endurance, balance and AI behaviour. Engineers see it as a step towards wider use.

stories.title: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
stories.summary: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.

stories.title: Hormuz standoff reignites as the IRGC appears to now shape Iran’s decisions
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz is blocked again by Iran as the IRGC’s hardline position appears to prevail in Tehran, setting the stage for the 3-day countdown towards the end of the ceasefire, with no new talks in sight.

stories.title: Watch: Obama and Mamdani sing with children in New York
stories.summary: The former US president and New York City mayor meet for the first time at a child care centre in the Bronx.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
stories.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

stories.title: Thousands of Parisians evacuated as WW2 bomb detonated
stories.summary: Residents within a 450m radius of the device were told to leave their homes while it was neutralised.

stories.title: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

stories.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
stories.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

stories.title: Widespread damage as storm spreads through midwestern US
stories.summary: A series of strong winds has torn through the midwestern US, ripping roofs off homes and leaving roads obstructed.

stories.title: Two police officers suspended after fleeing Kyiv shooting scene
stories.summary: An inquiry was opened after a video showed officers fleeing in panic from Saturday’s shooting, which left six dead and many injured. The patrol police chief has resigned.

stories.title: Mark Rutte nem látja jelét annak, hogy az Egyesült Államok otthagyná a NATO-t
stories.summary: Mark Rutte arról is beszélt, hogy Irán Európa biztonságát is fenyegeti. „Évtizedek óta Irán a káosz exportőre, nemcsak a saját régiójában, hanem világszerte” – mondta a Die Weltnek.

stories.title: ‘I was fighting two wars’: Ukraine’s soldiers confront their addiction struggles
stories.summary: Troops frequently use substances to help cope with untreated PTSD and anxiety, producing a negative spiral Seven years clean, Oleksandr believed he had left addiction behind. Then, a year into fighting Russia, the Ukrainian soldier was prescribed painkillers for a shoulder injury. Under the strain of war, he relapsed and quickly began using stronger illicit opioids. “From that moment, I was fighting two wars – one inside myself and one with Russia,” he said, speaking at a rehabilitation facility

stories.title: Iran closes strait of Hormuz again ‘until US lifts blockade’
stories.summary: IRGC reportedly fires on tanker as it tries to pass through strait during brief window when shipping lane had reopened Analysis: Trump and Tehran’s series of mismanaged posts stall progress towards peace Iranian officials say they have reversed the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and reimposed restrictions on the vital shipping lane after the US said it would not end its blockade of Iranian ports. A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a

stories.title: Pope Leo plays down rift with Trump, doubles down on peace
stories.summary: En route to Angola for a three-day visit, Pope Leo XIV said he has no desire to debate with US President Donald Trump. The two leaders have traded barbs since the pope spoke out against the Iran war.

stories.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
stories.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

stories.title: Ukraine: Gunman shot dead after killing spree in Kyiv
stories.summary: The mass shooting, in a busy central district of the Ukrainian capital, left 14 others wounded, officials said.

stories.title: Spain, Brazil, Mexico vow to boost Cuba aid amid US threats
stories.summary: The pledge comes amid a US oil embargo and Donald Trump's threats to invade the island. Leftist leaders meeting in Barcelona also spoke against the global far-right threat.

stories.title: Bodies of 50 babies, 6 adults found in Trinidad and Tobago
stories.summary: Police made the grim discovery at a cemetery in Cumuto on the island of Trinidad. Initial probes suggest it was a case of "unlawful disposal of unclaimed corpses."

stories.title: Building of Trump's White House ballroom can resume in full, appeals court says
stories.summary: Construction at the White House is expected to continue until the next hearing, due in June.

stories.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum denies 'diplomatic crisis' with Spain after conquest row
stories.summary: The Mexican president says there were never tensions over Spanish colonisation, which had become a thorny issue.

stories.title: The South Korean authors rising above a tide of hate to become bestsellers
stories.summary: A quiet revolution is unfolding as women writers carve out space in the wake of an anti-feminist backlash.

stories.title: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.title: Alec Baldwin to face civil trial over Rust film set shooting
stories.summary: The criminal case against the actor was dismissed in 2024.

stories.title: 'Croatia, but cheaper': The quirky holiday spots on trend for 2026
stories.summary: Montenegro and Albania are among the places rising in popularity for Britons seeking a holiday away from tourist traps.

stories.title: Harry and Meghan's trip felt like a royal tour - except many Aussies weren't interested
stories.summary: The Sussexes' four-day tour of Australia appears to have fallen flat with some.

stories.title: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

stories.title: Iran says deal still 'far off' as Hormuz Strait remains shut
stories.summary: Amid stalled US-Iran negotiations, Iranian officials warning the crucial shipping lane will remain shut until the US lifts its blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: North Korea conducts multiple ballistic missile tests
stories.summary: North Korea fired several short-range ballistic missiles into the East Sea on Sunday, the latest in a series of recent weapons tests that analysts say signal Pyongyang's rejection of South Korea's attempts to ease tensions.

stories.title: Öt éven belül a nyolcadik választást tartják Bulgáriában, amitől a politikai válság végét remélik
stories.summary: A legtöbb szavazatot az előrejelzések szerint a volt államfő, Rumen Radev új, baloldali pártszövetsége kaphatja majd.

stories.title: Élvezze ki a tavaszt, a nap végére jön a hidegfront
stories.summary: Késő estére 11-17 fokot mérhetünk a napközbeni 21-25 helyett.

stories.title: Van valami a levegőben: a héten minden lottósorsoláson volt telitalálat
stories.summary: A héten behúzták a Skandináv lottó, a hatos lottó, az ötös lottó és a Joker főnyereményét is.

stories.title: Debrecentől is kikapott, menesztették a Diósgyőr vezetőedzőjét
stories.summary: A DVTK három év élvonalbeli tagság után int búcsút az NB I.-nek.

stories.title: A tökéletes káposztasaláta trükkjei
stories.summary: Nem a gyorsétteremlánc titkos receptje, hanem egy teljesen hétköznapi eszköz, és pár apró trükk kell a tökéletes, ropogós és krémes káposztasalátához.

stories.title: Rendszerszintű egyenlőtlenségek, orosz befolyás, kampány állami pénzből – ilyen volt a választás a II. civil választási jelentés szerint
stories.summary: Civil szervezetek értékelték a választást és a kampányt, azt írták: a kampányfinanszírozás átláthatatlansága, a médiapluralizmus hiányosságai és a választási jogorvoslatok korlátozott működése hozzájárultak ahhoz, hogy a politikai verseny feltételei ne legyenek egyenlőek.

stories.title: Nem elég, hogy ez életed legrosszabb pillanata, de valaki még videóra is vette
stories.summary: Oscar-jelölt film Ethan Hawke főszereplésével, a Balhé második évada, kisvárosi horror és Aubrey Plaza animációs vígjátéka a heti streamingajánlónkban.

stories.title: Irán és az Egyesült Államok továbbra sem tud megegyezni nukleáris kérdésekben
stories.summary: A Hormuzi-szoros ügyében is vannak nézeteltérések, Irán újra teljes szigorú ellenőrzés alá vette a vízi átjárót.

stories.title: Kocsis Máté szerint nem tiltatta le a Hír TV-s interjút, baki történt, majd a Fidesz saját kis árulóiról és pénzéhes potyautasokról írt
stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezetője azt írta, „sok hiba, bűn és árulás történt, és sok sunyi, pénzéhes potyautas utazott” a Fidesszel.

stories.title: A NIS olajvállalat jövőjéről tárgyalt a szerb energiaügyi miniszter a Mol vezetőjével
stories.summary: Belgrádban találkoztak, a NIS jövőbeni működéséről és tulajdonosi helyzetéről egyeztettek. A miniszter szerint a megállapodás nem könnyű, és vannak olyan vörös vonalak, amiket Szerbia nem léphet át.

stories.title: Ukránellenes üzeneteket posztolt, korábban orosz bankszámlája és telefonszáma is volt a kijevi terrortámadás elkövetőjének
stories.summary: Az 58 éves férfi hat embert ölt meg. Egy szupermarketben barikádozta el magát, ahol túszokat is ejtett, végül a rendőrök lelőtték.

stories.title: A 13. kerület polgármestere szerint a Margitsziget státuszát helyre kell állítani
stories.summary: Tóth József azt is mondta, hogy az önkormányzati törvényhez valamilyen formában hozzá kell majd nyúlni.

stories.title: Mit gondol egy 19 éves fiatal a Szőlő utcáról?
stories.summary: Z generációs novelláskötet jelent meg, ami betekintést nyújt abba, hogyan is képzelik el a fiatalok a jövőt. De kik azok, akik egyáltalán ilyesmikről írni tudnak? Kritika.

stories.title: A pápa azt mondta, nem Trumpot zsarnokozta le a beszédében
stories.summary: Szerinte csak az emberek értelmezték félre azt, hogy Trumpra gondolt, amikor a világot feldúló zsarnokokról beszélt.

stories.title: A Szerencsejáték Zrt. szerint legközelebb talán nyolc év múlva történhet meg, hogy egy héten belül vigyenek el minden lottónyereményt
stories.summary: A Szerencsejáték Zrt. arra a pletykára is reagált, hogy nem véletlenül volt pont a választás utáni héten telitalálat az összes sorsoláson.

stories.title: Patkánymérget találtak a HiPP egyik bébiételében Ausztriában, több mint ezer üzletből hívták vissza a terméket
stories.summary: A HiPP szerint a súlyos helyzet „külső beavatkozás nyomán” alakult ki, és a Spar ausztriai forgalmazói hálózatát érinti.

stories.title: A kocsmák gyógyírt jelentenek a magányra
stories.summary: Legalábbis a baszk irodalom alapműve szerint, amelyben a szürreális keveredik a mágikus realizmussal, a humor a tragikummal, és amelyben többek között az írói eredetiség kérdései is felvetődnek.

stories.title: Majka Kocsis Máténak és Rákay Philipnek üzent, akik szerinte még mindig nem értik
stories.summary: Azt írta, hogy a bicskanyitogató pofátlanságnak bicskanyitogatás a vége.

stories.title: Gyurcsány Ferenc is értékelte a választás eredményét
stories.summary: Szerinte aki veri a mellét, hogy csak neki van igaza és erre épít politikai rendszert, az lehet, hogy első lépésben képes kiirtani a riválisait, de végül önmagát is felszámolja.

stories.title: Hétfőn tárgyal újra Amerika és Irán, Trump azt ígéri, nem lesz rendes srác, ha Irán nem adja be a derekát
stories.summary: „No more, Mr. Nice Guy” – ígérte az amerikai elnök, aki azt mondta, az is lehet, hogy fel fog robbantani minden hidat.

stories.title: Semmilyen fogamzásgátló sem kapható a Budai Irgalmasrendi Kórház patikájában a „rend szellemisége miatt”
stories.summary: A patikában dolgozó gyógyszertáros szerint „a rendi szellemiség” miatt nem tartanak fogamzásgátlót az üzletben.

stories.title: Milyen messze vagyunk az eurótól?
stories.summary: Tavaly egyetlen feltételt sem teljesítettünk, így ezek 2030-as elérése komoly vállalás lenne az új kormánytól.

stories.title: Az Eufória innen már nehezen fog visszajönni
stories.summary: Kutyapózban ugató Sydney Sweeney-vel és drogfutárkodó Zendayával tért vissza több tragikus haláleset és háttérben zajló konfliktus után Sam Levinson sorozata, ami mostanra már inkább önmaga paródiájába hajlik.

stories.title: Szoboszlai gólpasszával nyerte meg a Liverpool a városi rangadót
stories.summary: Kerkez Milos is pályára lépett a meccsen.

stories.title: Borbás Marcsi reagált Kocsis Máté árulózására: A vád méltatlan, bízom benne, hogy visszahull a vádló fejére
stories.summary: „Ami pedig az anyagiak hamis színben való feltüntetését illeti, az ügyvédünknek már ismerős pálya lesz” – írta a műsorvezető.

stories.title: Elütötték a fekete bárányt, aki körülnézés nélkül rohant át az úton
stories.summary: Két lába is eltörött.

stories.title: Az amerikai energiaügyi miniszter szerint a benzin ára akár 2027-ig magas maradhat
stories.summary: Donald Trump amerikai elnök arról beszélt, hogy az üzemanyagárak a novemberi amerikai félidős választásokig „lehet, hogy változatlanok maradnak, vagy talán kicsit emelkednek.”

stories.title: Praktikus, használtan 2 millió forint körül elérhető, de jár némi kockázat a Dacia Sanderóval
stories.summary: Az utastere pofonegyszerű, cserébe tartós és könnyen takarítható. A motorok között találunk többféle benzinest és dízelt is, ezért összegyűjtöttük, hogy mi mindenre kell ügyelni a vásárlás előtt.

stories.title: Nyolc gyerek halt meg egy louisianai lövöldözésben
stories.summary: A gyerekek közül többen a lövöldözéssel gyanúsított személy rokonai voltak.

stories.title: Wáberer György Kocsis Máténak: Én saját hatáskörömben irtottam a korrupciót, amivel ki is vívtam fideszes politikusok ellenszenvét
stories.summary: Az üzletember is reagált arra, hogy Kocsis Facebook-posztjában árulónak nevezte.

stories.title: A belpolitikai helyzetre hivatkozva hátrált ki a Nobel-díjas Krausz Ferenc alapítványa egy sikeres tehetséggondozó program mögül
stories.summary: Novemberben jelentették be, hogy a Krausz vezette Élvonal veszi át a 14 éve nagy sikerrel működő Nemzeti Tudósképző Akadémia támogatását. A Nobel-díjas tudós most a politikai helyzetre hivatkozik, és a minisztériumtól tehetséggondozásra kapott támogatást a saját nagyberuházására kívánja költeni.

stories.title: Makranczi Zalán szerint a színházban dolgozó emberek félnek, rettegnek Vidnyánszky Attilától
stories.summary: A színész szerint Vidnyánszkynak vissza kellene lépnie az összes hatalmi pozíciójából, hogy megszűnjön a félelem.

stories.title: Borzasztó körülmények közül mentettek kutyákat az állatvédők, egy 11 hónapos kislányra bukkantak közben
stories.summary: A gyerek koszos volt, a gyermekvédelmisek azonnal kiemelték a kislányt, akire a földön fekve találtak rá az állatvédők.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt leendő egészségügyi miniszter: Július 1-ig leszereltetjük a kórházi arcfelismerőket
stories.summary: Nemcsak a választás estéjén előadott tánca, hanem az egészségügy megreformálása miatt is szeretne híres lenni.

stories.title: Venezuela’s Machado to hold Madrid rally as opposition frozen out after Maduro capture
stories.summary: Exiled leader to revive push for change amid US backing of Delcy Rodríguez and delays to democratic transition Venezuela’s opposition leader, María Corina Machado, will seek to revive her push for political change with a rally in Madrid on Saturday, having found herself sidelined by Donald Trump after the abduction of the president Nicolás Maduro. “Venezuela will be free,” the Nobel peace prize winner insisted in an interview on the eve of this weekend’s demonstration in the Puerta del Sol squar

stories.title: BBC reports from Lebanese border town as residents try to return home
stories.summary: BBC Arabic's correspondent Carine Torbey reports from the border town of Khiam, as the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon goes into its second day.

stories.title: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
stories.summary: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

stories.title: Sanctions don’t result in regime change. Whether against Iran or Russia, western countries need shrewder tactics | Simon Jenkins
stories.summary: Dissent cannot take hold in soil rendered barren by emigration and embargo. Political opposition needs academic and cultural exchange if it is to prosper The chancellor of the exchequer and the IMF agree . Britain’s economy is about to take its greatest hit for decades. This is collateral damage from the US’s war on Iran and the closing of the strait of Hormuz – and will be made worse by sanctioning Gulf oil exports . Britain has already been weakened by four years of sanctions against Russia ov

stories.title: Lebanon truce takes effect as damaged Qasmiyeh bridge reopens
stories.summary: Lebanon and Israel entered a 10-day truce early Friday, as the army reopened a single lane on the damaged Qasmiyeh Bridge in the south after Israeli strikes hit the key crossing linking Tyre to the wider region.

stories.title: Inside smoky shelters, a fast-paced, illegal card game has taken off in Solomon Islands
stories.summary: Growing numbers in the capital Honiara are playing the street card game Pass for a chance of a big payout, while risking big losses As the school day ends in Honiara, *Irene, a 43-year-old teacher in a floral dress with a yellow daisy in her bun, steps on to a minibus. After 10 minutes, Irene gets off the bus, walks down an alley, and enters a damp, smoky shelter. Plastic tables fill the space and playing cards are scattered on the floor. Irene has stopped by a hidden gambling table in a western

stories.title: Ülés alól kicsúszó vécét rakna az autóiba egy kínai gyártó
stories.summary: A Seres elektromos autókat gyárt, és úgy tűnik, furcsa extrát szeretne kínálni a vásárlóinak.

stories.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
stories.summary: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

stories.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
stories.summary: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

stories.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

stories.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
stories.summary: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

stories.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
stories.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

stories.title: Watch: Fuel truck leaves trail of fire on road in Syria
stories.summary: Eyewitnesses captured the moment a burning fuel tanker drove through the centre of Hasakah, Syria, leaving a trail of fire behind it.

stories.title: Iranian gunboats fire on tanker transiting the Strait of Hormuz, UK military says
stories.summary: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations Centre says two gunboats from Iran’s Revolutionary Guard opened fire on a tanker transiting the Strait of Hormuz after Iran said it had reimposed restrictions on the vital waterway.

stories.title: Israel accuses Hezbollah militants of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: A French UN peace worker has been killed in southern Lebanon as Israel accuses Hezbollah militants of breaching the 10-day ceasefire with Hezbollah.

stories.title: Starmer faces calls to step down after appointing Epstein associate
stories.summary: Opposition leaders demanded Starmer's resignation on Thursday after it emerged that US envoy Peter Mandelson was appointed despite having failed his security check.

stories.title: Madonna joins Sabrina Carpenter to surprise Coachella
stories.summary: The Queen of Pop makes a guest appearance during the Espresso singer's headline set.

stories.title: Olive harvesting at the Colosseum: a plantation in Roman ruins in the heart of the city
stories.summary: There are about 2,200 farms within the city limits of Italy’s capital alone, making it the largest agricultural municipality in Europe.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV lands in Angola, says it is 'not in my interest at all' to debate Trump
stories.summary: After visiting Cameroon, Angola is the third leg of Pope Leo XIV's 11-day tour of Africa. People hope for appeals for peace and for him to tackle the economic woes of the oil- and rare-earth-rich nation.

stories.title: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
stories.summary: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

stories.title: More than half of Britons support rejoining EU 10 years on from Brexit vote
stories.summary: Experts say Labour’s ‘halfway house’ approach risks losing support from progressives and ‘red wall’ voters Support for rejoining the EU rather than simply rejoining the single market is growing among British voters, with more than 80% of Labour, Liberal Democrat and Green party supporters favouring this option, according to research mapping voter attitudes 10 years after the Brexit referendum. Labour’s “muted” approach to the issue means it risks losing support among progressive voters and in “r

stories.title: Jacinta Allan wants voters to see Victoria’s 12-year-old Labor government as ‘new and united’. Can she cling to power?
stories.summary: With a reshuffled cabinet, the premier is hoping to quell leadership rumblings as her party seeks an unprecedented fourth term Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast As the Victorian premier, Jacinta Allan, stood alongside the fresh faces in her reshuffled cabinet on Wednesday, she attempted to send her increasingly jaded electorate a blunt message: despite its 12 years in power, her government is – apparently – new. In her opening four-minute preamble to reporters, Allan -

stories.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
stories.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

stories.title: Ankara pushes deeper economic ties with Astana ahead of Erdoğan visit
stories.summary: Astana and Ankara aim to boost trade, diversify exports and launch joint industrial projects ahead of Erdoğan’s state visit to Kazakhstan, setting a goal of $15 billion in trade volumes.

stories.title: Military operation against Hezbollah 'still not complete,' Israel's defence minister says
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump announced a 10-day ceasefire on Friday after a meeting between the Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors, the first direct diplomatic talks between the two countries in decades.

stories.title: Rome and Caucasus poised to further discuss closer energy ties
stories.summary: The Italian prime minister is expected to visit Azerbaijan in the coming weeks to continue political dialogue and energy cooperation between Rome and Baku, with Azerbaijani gas supplies via the Trans Adriatic Pipeline remaining a key pillar of bilateral ties.

stories.title: ‘How do I end a call?’: the elderly Japanese people determined to master smartphones
stories.summary: Elderly people take advantage of courses on how to navigate mobile devices and avoid ‘analogue isolation’ It’s not only young people whose gaze is fixed on tiny screens. But for these users in Tokyo, clicking and scrolling is anything but second nature. “I can’t deal with all of the apps that jump out at me,” says one. “How do I know if I’ve definitely ended a call?” asks another. Continue reading...

stories.title: Home Office ‘red flag’ error leaves German mother separated from toddler in UK
stories.summary: Liza Tobay was told settled status had been ‘red flagged’ when she tried to fly home from Germany to Scotland A German woman has been separated from her two-year-old daughter in Edinburgh after a Home Office mistake left her stranded in Dusseldorf earlier this week. Liza Tobay, who has lived in the UK for 15 years, had taken her oldest child, a six-year-old boy, to visit his grandfather and some other relatives over Easter when confronted with what she said appeared to be “a serious administrati

stories.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
stories.summary: Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions. Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so

stories.title: Kenyan firm sacks more than 1,000 workers after losing Meta contract
stories.summary: Meta paused work with Sama last month after allegations about staff viewing private scenes filmed by smart glasses More than 1,000 low-paid workers in Kenya have been abruptly sacked by an outsourcing company contracted by Meta, in what activists said was a shocking move exposing the precariousness of tech jobs in the global south. Sama, a company based in Nairobi to which Meta outsourced content moderation and AI training work, announced on Thursday that the workers were being laid off after Me

stories.title: India fails to pass bill to boost women’s representation after delimitation row
stories.summary: Opposition accuses Narendra Modi government of using quotas as cover for redrawing electoral map The Indian government has failed to pass a bill to increase female representation in parliament after being accused of using the plan as a guise to redraw the country’s electoral map. It was the first time in 12 years in power that a constitutional amendment proposed by Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) government was not passed by parliament. Continue reading...

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Crisis in the Middle East, Russian strikes in Kyiv, Orthodox Easter and Karol G at Coachella – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
stories.summary: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

stories.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
stories.summary: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

stories.title: Cyprus: Global politics felt at Candidates chess tournaments
stories.summary: The FIDE Candidates tournaments are two of the most important events in the chess calendar. This year, they took place in Cyprus. But tension relating to Russia and the war in the Middle East was never too far away.

stories.title: Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope
stories.summary: The war in Iran and a spat between two of the world's most influential Americans has forced Giorgia Meloni to choose sides, potentially pushing the Italian prime minister to align herself with Europe's center-right.

stories.title: US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon goes into effect
stories.summary: Trump extended an invitation to Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to hold the first direct meeting at that level in decades.

stories.title: 'Ripple effects of Iran war could lead to other conflicts,' UN development chief tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the UN Development Programme told Euronews that the impact of the Middle East conflict is being felt "way beyond the region", and that, in the absence of a resolution, the groundwork for further conflicts is being laid.

stories.title: Cameroon sees mass turnout for Pope Leo XIV visit as he calls for peace
stories.summary: Cameroon saw thousands of faithful gather on Wednesday 16 April at Bamenda’s airfield, where Pope Leo XIV celebrated an open‑air Mass and called for a “decisive change of course” towards peace.

stories.title: Netherlands Keukenhof draws global visitors for spring display
stories.summary: Netherlands opened its Keukenhof garden in Lisse for the 2026 spring season, welcoming visitors to an eight-week display featuring around seven million flowers.

stories.title: Escaped wolf Neukgu captured after nine-day search in South Korea
stories.summary: A two-year-old wolf that escaped from O-World zoo in Daejeon, South Korea, has been safely recaptured after spending nine days on the loose. The male wolf, Neukgu, was born at the zoo in 2024 and is part of an initiative to restore wolves resembling those once native to the Korean peninsula.

stories.title: China and Russia ties driving North Korean economic recovery, Seoul ministry says
stories.summary: China has long been the diplomatically isolated country's main economic backer, though Pyongyang has also drawn closer to Moscow since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

stories.title: BBC in Iran: 'Tehran does not think it has lost this war'
stories.summary: Lyse Doucet says Iranians want a solution to the long-running animosity with the US, but leaders is not willing to make a deal on Washington's terms.

stories.title: Three charged over attempted arson on Persian-language TV channel, UK police say
stories.summary: Iran International is one of several Persian‑language outlets critical of Iran's clerical leadership operating from abroad.

stories.title: Germany’s Affordable Art Fair draws crowds with artworks under €500
stories.summary: Germany is hosting the Affordable Art Fair Berlin from 16 to 19 April 2026. The event brings together dozens of galleries and focuses on artworks priced under €500, aiming to broaden access to contemporary art.

stories.title: Nearly 900 Rohingya refugees dead and missing in shipwrecks in 2025, UNHCR says
stories.summary: The Rohingya mainly leave from huge camps in Bangladesh, where more than 1 million refugees forced to flee across the border from Myanmar's Rakhine state live in squalid conditions.

stories.title: Italy: 25 hostages freed as Naples bank gang vanishes underground
stories.summary: A group of three masked robbers held 25 people hostage during a bank heist in Naples on Thursday 16 April 2026 before escaping through a tunnel connected to the city’s sewer network, authorities said.

stories.title: Global sigh of relief and mixed messages as US and Iran say Strait of Hormuz open
stories.summary: Iran and US declare Strait of Hormuz "completely open," but Iran adds that passage is on a "coordinated route" set by Tehran.

stories.title: Venezuela and EU set out roadmap for renewed ties amid political thaw
stories.summary: Following talks in the Venezuelan parliament, Caracas and the EU have agreed on a new cooperation agenda. The shift aligns with a broader economic pivot, as the nation resumes ties with the IMF and the World Bank.

stories.title: 'I understand when you say no to war,' Brazil's Lula tells Spain's PM Pedro Sánchez
stories.summary: Lula and Sánchez are both outspoken in their criticism of US President Donald Trump, who has threatened both with punitive tariffs.

stories.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire: civilians return to ruined towns in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: A ceasefire in Lebanon has brought a pause in fighting after weeks of war. Residents are returning to damaged homes as questions remain over how long the truce will hold.

stories.title: White House vs the pope: What is behind the clash and Catholic just war doctrine?
stories.summary: Theologians Euronews spoke to believe that the escalating war of words between Washington and the Holy See has raised important questions over Catholic and Christian moral thought.

stories.title: First cruise ship transits the Strait of Hormuz since Iran war began
stories.summary: Iran and the US said the strategic waterway, which has been effectively closed since the beginning of the war, will be fully open to commercial traffic for the remainder of the ceasefire

stories.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
stories.summary: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

stories.title: US blockade of Iranian ports to last 'as long as it takes,' Defence Secretary Hegseth says
stories.summary: The US naval blockade of Iran’s ports came into effect on Monday, with President Donald Trump warning that any attack ships would be “eliminated” if they attempted to break it.

stories.title: About 250 missing after boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsizes in Andaman Sea
stories.summary: Trawler set off from Bangladesh and reportedly capsized due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding About 250 people are missing after a boat carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea, according to the UN’s refugee and migration agencies. The agencies said the trawler carrying more than 250 men, women and children reportedly sank due to harsh weather and overcrowding. It had departed from Teknaf in southern Bangladesh and was bound for Malaysia. Continu

stories.title: US launches fifth strike on alleged Pacific drug boat in a week, killing three
stories.summary: Wednesday’s strike brings the total of those killed in US military strikes on alleged drug boats to at least 177 Three people were killed in a US strike on another alleged drug-trafficking boat, the fifth such deadly attack in as many days, military officials have announced. US southern command said it conducted “a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations” in the eastern Pacific, without naming the alleged group, in an X post. Continue reading...

stories.title: 'Everything flew': Ukrainians describe Russian attack on apartment block – video
stories.summary: Russian strikes killed at least 12 people in Ukraine , local authorities have said, after Moscow pummelled its neighbour during the night with drones and missiles. In Odesa, Russian strikes killed six people, according to the head of the city’s military administration. Strikes on the capital, Kyiv, killed at least four people, including a 12-year-old, the state emergency service of Ukraine said, while another two people died in the central city of Dnipro, according to Oleksandr Ganzha, the head

stories.title: Spain: Sanchez government finalizes amnesty plan for undocumented migrants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government hailed the plan as "an act of justice and a necessity." It will allow unauthorized migrants to apply for temporary residence permits instead, and to start paying taxes.

stories.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
stories.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.
